US navy helicopter makes emergency water landing in Arabian Sea; one crew member missing
india.summary: A US Navy Sea Hawk helicopter made an emergency water landing in the Arabian Sea early Tuesday, with one crew member still missing. Three of the four crew members were rescued and are in stable condition. The US Navy is conducting search operations for the missing individual. The cause of the incident is under investigation, with no indication of hostile action amid regional tensions.

india.title: Turkish proverb of the day: 'Kill the brave man, but do not deny him his due'
india.summary: The Turkish proverb, 'Kill the brave man, but do not deny him his due,' emphasizes the importance of recognizing the courage and merit of an opponent, even in moments of competition. This age-old wisdom speaks volumes about the need for fairness and integrity in both personal and professional interactions. It teaches us that true strength lies in appreciating the virtues of others, fostering growth and mutual respect within society.

india.title: Swedish proverb of the day: 'Even a blind hen finds a grain sometimes'
india.summary: The age-old proverb, even a blind hen occasionally finds a grain, underscores the impact of luck on success. Originating from agricultural traditions, it serves as a gentle reminder that individual victories don't always equate to expertise. This saying promotes humility and recognizes that both luck and hard work contribute to our achievements, echoing its relevance in contemporary society by encouraging us to refrain from hasty judgments based on singular milestones.

business.title: US Federal Reserve chair Kevin Warsh says he will stick by 2% inflation target, vows to bring in real-time economic data for making interest rate decisions
business.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh declared unwavering commitment to the 2% inflation target, signaling no monetary policy easing despite President Trump's calls for rate cuts. Warsh emphasized the Fed's independence and announced plans to leverage real-time economic data.

business.title: Uber-backed Lime rides IPO wave to $1.73 billion valuation in Nasdaq debut
business.summary: Uber-backed Lime jumped 8% in its Nasdaq debut, valuing the e-scooter and bike rental company at $1.73 billion. The IPO reflects renewed investor interest in new listings amid volatile markets. Lime, operating in 230+ cities, remains loss-making but benefits from growing urban micromobility demand and Uber partnership support.

business.summary: Indian equities rebounded after two sessions of losses, with Sensex rising 444 points and Nifty reclaiming 24,000. Easing geopolitical tensions, trade deal optimism and lower oil prices boosted sentiment, while analysts maintained a bullish outlook above the key 23,800 support.

business.title: From WhatsApp chats to food orders: How Sebi cracked a Rs 144 crore stock manipulation scheme
business.summary: Sebi uncovered an alleged Rs 144 crore pump-and-dump scheme by analysing airline bookings, hotel stays, food delivery records, WhatsApp chats, telecom data and banking trails. The investigation linked digital footprints to identify the alleged mastermind and entities behind five manipulated stocks.

business.title: Shutterstock shares crash 29% after Getty Images calls off $3.7 billion merger
business.summary: Shutterstock shares plunged nearly 30% after Getty Images scrapped its $3.7 billion merger following UK regulator objections. The Competition and Markets Authority required divestment of Shutterstock’s editorial business, leading Getty to walk away. The collapse hit investor sentiment as both firms now face rising competition from AI-generated image tools.

business.title: US Stocks: Meta building cloud business to sell AI computing, says report; shares rise
business.summary: Meta Platforms is reportedly exploring a move into the cloud computing business, aiming to lease out its surplus AI computing power. This strategic shift could lessen Meta's dependence on advertising revenue and position it as a competitor to tech giants like Amazon and Microsoft.

business.title: Retail space leasing in malls, high streets rises 18 pc to 2.4 mn sq ft across top 8 cities: Cushman
business.summary: Retailers are witnessing an impressive 18% increase in leasing activities for shopping mall and high street locations across key Indian cities. Leading this surge are the fashion and food sectors, which are eagerly pursuing top-tier spaces despite the scarcity. This trend reflects a strong demand from consumers and suggests exciting prospects for further retail expansion as additional spaces become accessible.

business.title: Kashmir apple growers question viability of CA storage as rising costs, weak prices squeeze returns
business.summary: The returns this year failed to justify the additional expenses, say farmers

business.title: Micron, GM sign semiconductor supply agreement for vehicles
business.summary: General Motors has signed a landmark long-term agreement with Micron Technology to ensure a steady supply of vital memory and storage chips. This partnership comes in response to the increasing demand and costs for these crucial components, fueled by advancements in AI. Moreover, it sets the stage for joint innovation in future automotive technologies, underscoring GM's commitment to strengthening its supply chain resilience.

business.title: Fed's Warsh: Will decide on rate hike when policymakers 'shut the door' at next meeting
business.summary: At an international monetary policy forum, Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh stressed the necessity of maintaining a 2% inflation target. He notably refrained from giving any forward guidance on interest rates. Warsh's remarks have sparked debate among economists regarding potential future rate increases, as global central banks adopt varying approaches to tackle inflation and uphold the independence of the Federal Reserve.

business.title: Air Marshal Dixit takes over as IAF’s Vice Chief
business.summary: Now Air Marshal Tejinder Singh has been tasked to take over as the Chief of Integrated Defence Staff to the Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee (CISC)

business.title: US Stocks: Nike shares fall on grim sales outlook; China woes hit turnaround plans
business.summary: Shares of Nike fell as investors raised alarms over the slow pace of recovery with CEO Elliott Hill at the helm for nearly two years. While the company managed a slight revenue uptick, it suffered a 17% decline in sales in China, expected to worsen. Analysts highlight a sluggish revival in both the sportswear sector and international markets, forecasting no comeback until fiscal 2028.

business.title: Nithin Kamath reveals Zerodha's playbook: No ads, no sales targets, customer-first approach
business.summary: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath said the brokerage's growth stems from prioritising customer interests over aggressive expansion. He highlighted transparency, word-of-mouth marketing, a bootstrapped model and product-focused innovation as the key drivers behind the company's long-term success.

business.title: US stocks today: US stocks dip as US-Iran tensions weigh on sentiment
business.summary: US stocks fell as US-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment, dragging Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq lower. Tech stocks led declines even as Meta surged on AI cloud plans. Investors also tracked Fed rate hike risks, weak manufacturing data, and upcoming US jobs report amid cautious start to second half.

business.title: Pump-and-dump operation: Sebi bans 221 entities for up to 7 years; Hanif Shekh fined Rs 10 cr
business.summary: Sebi has taken significant action against a large-scale pump-and-dump operation, imposing a barring on 221 parties, including the alleged ringleader Hanif Shekh, from participating in the securities market for up to seven years. An imposing fine of Rs 10 crore was also levied. Between 2017 and 2020, this illegal scheme manipulated five stocks, artificially inflating their prices and attracting unwitting investors, ultimately resulting in illicit gains of Rs 143.79 crore.

business.title: Quote of the day by Edwin Lefevre: "It is not the certainty of disaster ahead but the uncertainty of better days to come that keeps the investor from buying"
business.summary: Financial wisdom highlights that markets often reward those who act amidst uncertainty. Waiting for absolute clarity during downturns can lead to missed opportunities as stock prices recover. History shows strong rallies often begin with weak sentiment. Successful investing hinges on discipline, focusing on long-term fundamentals rather than short-term fears. Uncertainty, therefore, presents a chance to acquire quality assets at good prices for superior returns.
